sanskrit: अश्रद्दधानाः पुरुषा धर्मस्यास्य परन्तप । अप्राप्य मां निवर्तन्ते मृत्युसंसारवर्त्मनि ॥
hindiTranslation: हे परंतप इस धर्मकी महिमापर श्रद्धा न रखनेवाले मनुष्य मेरे प्राप्त न होकर मृत्युरूप संसारके मार्गमें लौटते रहते हैं अर्थात् बारबार जन्मतेमरते रहते हैं ॥
englishTranslation: O conqueror of foes, people without trust in this dharma do not reach Me and keep returning on the path of repeated death and rebirth.
essence: Without trust, the highest teaching cannot carry you beyond repetition.
speaker: Krishna
simpleMeaning: People who lack trust in this sacred teaching do not reach Krishna and remain trapped in repeated birth and death.
simpleInsight: Trust is not decoration here; it is the doorway out of repetition.
detailedMeaning: Krishna is still speaking to Arjuna on the battlefield, where a whole life is being decided in one frozen moment. After praising this teaching as the highest and purest knowledge, he warns that disbelief keeps a person turning in the cycle of death and rebirth instead of reaching him.
modernRelevance: You stand at the edge of a hard choice and keep asking for one more guarantee, one more sign. Doubt can become a loop that sends you back to the same fear instead of forward into action.
translationLiteral: अश्रद्दधानाः (without trust) / पुरुषाः (people) / धर्मस्य (of dharma) / अस्य (of this) / परन्तप (O conqueror of foes) / अप्राप्य (not reaching) / माम् (Me) / निवर्तन्ते (return) / मृत्युसंसारवर्त्मनि (on the path of the cycle of death and rebirth)
